channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Hey, Siri: Apple just announced a long-awaited AI update
item.description: At its annual developers' conference, Apple put the spotlight on new AI features, while highlighting security and child safety — and critiquing the company's AI competitors.
item.content:encoded:
At its annual developers' conference, Apple put the spotlight on new AI features, while highlighting security and child safety — and critiquing the company's AI competitors.

(Image credit: Noah Berger)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: With just days left, the U.S. opening match at the World Cup is still not sold out
item.description: Hundreds of tickets are still available for the U.S. and Canada opening matches for the World Cup on Friday. Even more are available in resale platforms — many at below face value.
item.content:encoded:
Hundreds of tickets are still available for the U.S. and Canada opening matches for the World Cup on Friday. Even more are available in resale platforms — many at below face value.

(Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon)
item.dc:creator: Rafael Nam

item.title: NPR's new chief content officer: 'I've been training for this job my whole life'
item.description: Less than two weeks after overhauling its newsroom, NPR has hired Nadine Zylstra to be its chief content officer. She has been a top executive at Sesame Workshop, YouTube and Pinterest.
item.content:encoded:
Less than two weeks after overhauling its newsroom, NPR has hired Nadine Zylstra to be its chief content officer. She has been a top executive at Sesame Workshop, YouTube and Pinterest.

(Image credit: Variety via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Former '60 Minutes' correspondent Scott Pelley speaks out after firing
item.description: Former 60 Minutes correspondent Scott Pelley said CBS news leader Bari Weiss shouldn't be leading the network. Pelley spoke to The New York Times just days after being fired by CBS.
item.content:encoded: Former 60 Minutes correspondent Scott Pelley said CBS news leader Bari Weiss shouldn't be leading the network. Pelley spoke to The New York Times just days after being fired by CBS.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Kalshi and Polymarket crack down on paid influencers claiming election fraud
item.description: Influencers are using prediction market odds to sow doubt in vote counting, in some cases in posts paid for by the companies themselves.
item.content:encoded:
Influencers are using prediction market odds to sow doubt in vote counting, in some cases in posts paid for by the companies themselves.

(Image credit: Jill Connelly)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: In the wake of layoffs, some tech workers are exploring new careers
item.description: Amid widespread tech layoffs, some highly skilled workers are making radical career changes. Some laid-off workers are turning to lower paying temp jobs, and some are leaving tech altogether.
item.content:encoded: Amid widespread tech layoffs, some highly skilled workers are making radical career changes. Some laid-off workers are turning to lower paying temp jobs, and some are leaving tech altogether.
item.dc:creator: Monica Nickelsburg

item.title: The U.S. adds 172,000 jobs. Many are in restaurants, bars and hotels
item.description: U.S. employers added jobs for the third month in a row in May, while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3%. But wage gains softened and likely failed to keep pace with rising prices.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. employers added jobs for the third month in a row in May, while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3%. But wage gains softened and likely failed to keep pace with rising prices.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: A global World Cup tradition catches fire in the U.S.: Panini sticker collecting
item.description: Collecting Panini stickers is a World Cup tradition beloved in Europe and Latin America. In the U.S., interest has been building steadily, and this summer, the buzz is bigger than ever.
item.content:encoded:
Collecting Panini stickers is a World Cup tradition beloved in Europe and Latin America. In the U.S., interest has been building steadily, and this summer, the buzz is bigger than ever.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: What do you actually get when you pay for AI?
item.description: Just 3% of U.S. households pay for AI for personal use. Sign ups are growing — even though Americans have subscription fatigue.
item.content:encoded:
Just 3% of U.S. households pay for AI for personal use. Sign ups are growing — even though Americans have subscription fatigue.

(Image credit: Charly Triballeau/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: How cellphone carriers prepare for hurricane season with AI, drones and 'cows'
item.description: Hurricane season is expected to be milder than usual this year. But that's not stopping cell phone companies from pulling out all the stops.
item.content:encoded:
Hurricane season is expected to be milder than usual this year. But that's not stopping cell phone companies from pulling out all the stops.

(Image credit: Melissa Sue Gerrits)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie
